title: senecio pascoensis
intro: Senecio pascoensis, also known as Pasco's ragwort or Pasco's groundsel, is a rare perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to the Columbia River Gorge in Oregon, USA. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and may be used to treat conditions like arthritis and menstrual cramps. Senecio pascoensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health issues.
